methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Mitra Chem is a Series B-stage climate and energy company founded by Vivas Kumar and Will Fitzgerald that has raised $60M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 9 companies (1 public, 8 private) operating in the same sector and stage, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach uses the $60M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $114M. The peer-median method examines six same-sector, same-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $120M to $5.0B; the median across this cohort is $355M. This wide range reflects material heterogeneity within the climate-tech peer set, particularly the presence of Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B.

Triangulating both methods produces a valuation range of $97M to $408M, with a median of $234M. The stage-progression result sits below peer median valuation, while peer median sits at the upper boundary of the methodological range. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the peer set given the $5.0B outlier (Commonwealth), the materiality of capital intensity and time-to-revenue differences across climate subsectors, and Mitra Chem's relative technological or market positioning versus named comparables. The $234M midpoint estimate carries material range compression risk.
